I’ve requested that the pallbearers jog the casket to the grave to keep things lively and ensure I make a grand, high-speed entrance into the afterlife.
I want a casket lined with neon faux-fur and perhaps a built-in cocktail shaker, just in case the afterlife has a strict happy hour policy.
Please make sure the eulogy focuses on my legendary ability to burn toast and that one time I accidentally joined a cult for a weekend.
I’m planning to have a professional clown distribute balloons at the wake, because nothing says "eternal rest" like a slightly panicked balloon animal.
I’ve decided on a glitter-bomb explosion triggered the moment the lid closes, giving me the ultimate last laugh on my way out.
I’ve found that the secret to a successful summit is ensuring the catering is lavish enough that the ministers are too full to start any actual wars.
My strategy for the trade talks is to smile vaguely and hope they don't realize I've accidentally traded three provinces for a shipment of artisanal cheese.
I treat the diplomatic protocol handbook as a series of daring suggestions rather than a set of rules, mostly to see who will flinch first.
If we just put all the world leaders in a room with a few board games, we’d solve the border disputes in an afternoon.
I love how "deeply concerned" is the diplomatic code for "we are currently staring at the ceiling in total panic."
I’m just hoping I don’t accidentally spill a full glass of red wine on their white shirt within the first ten minutes.
If the conversation hits a lull, I’ll probably just start describing my childhood pet rock in vivid detail to break the tension.
I chose this loud taco joint because it’s much harder for them to hear my nervous rambling over the sound of sizzling fajitas.
My current strategy for managing nerves is to pretend I'm a secret agent on a high-stakes mission to discover if they like pineapple on pizza.
If this goes south, I’ve already mentally rehearsed three different ways to fake a sudden, urgent phone call from my imaginary cat.
I’m pretty sure my lower back is just staging a protest against my decision to carry all the groceries in one trip.
My blood pressure is currently high enough to power a small village in the Alps.
I’ve got a cough that sounds like a seal trying to clear its throat after eating a bag of gravel.
My cholesterol levels are essentially a gourmet buffet for my arteries at this point.
I think my inner ear has decided to relocate to a different time zone, because the whole room is doing the cha-cha.
I’m pretty sure the priest is just winging it with those dramatic hand gestures to distract us from how long the sermon is actually taking.
I always treat the fasting period as a high-stakes game of "how many calories can I sneak in without the congregation noticing."
I love how the incense makes the whole cathedral smell like a fancy hotel lobby, provided you ignore the part where I can't actually breathe.
I spend most of the chanting sessions trying to figure out if I’m actually hitting the note or if I just sound like a dying goat.
I’ve mastered the art of looking profoundly enlightened during the silent meditation while actually just wondering if I left the oven on.
I’m just here to see which executive gets the fancy mahogany desk and who gets banished to the windowless basement.
Our synergy is basically just a fancy way of saying we’re going to fire half the accounting department.
I’ve spent three hours reading the due diligence report, and it’s essentially a very expensive list of everything they’ve messed up.
I can't wait for the "cultural integration" phase, where we pretend to like each other while fighting over the coffee machine.
I’m betting my entire bonus that the regulatory board finds a reason to kill this deal before Friday.
I’m currently playing a high-stakes game of "find the forbidden object" where the prize is my own sanity and the opponent is a tiny human with the stealth of a ninja.
Bedtime is essentially a psychological thriller where my child suddenly remembers they are starving and terrified of the darkness the moment their head hits the pillow.
I’ve discovered that the only way to get a toddler to eat a vegetable is to lie through my teeth and tell them it’s a magical dinosaur tree.
Watching a two-year-old have a complete meltdown because I peeled their banana "the wrong way" is a masterclass in absolute absurdity.
My living room now looks like a glitter bomb went off in a toy factory, and I’m pretty sure there’s a half-eaten cracker fused to the sofa.
I’m fairly certain the judge is only keeping me in contempt of court because he’s jealous of my flair for dramatic pauses.
My witness is currently treating the stand like a comedy club, and frankly, I’m wondering if I should charge admission.
I’ve spent so much time reviewing these discovery documents that I’ve started seeing footnotes in my sleep.
If the jury looks any more confused, I might have to break out the crayons and draw them a map of the crime scene.
I’m planning to make my closing argument so emotionally devastating that the court reporter will need a box of tissues and a nap.
I’m pretty sure the "freeze-dried ice cream" is actually just chalky nostalgia shaped like a cube.
If I accidentally leave the airlock open, I’ll finally find out if I actually sound like a tea kettle in a vacuum.
I’ve spent three million miles traveling just to realize that Mars is basically just a giant, dusty parking lot.
My favorite part of the training is pretending I’m a confused penguin while spinning in the centrifuge.
I can't wait to tell the aliens that we come in peace, provided they have a decent Wi-Fi signal.
